summary: This is a guest book review by contributing editor Brian Todoroff reviewing Taunton Press's 'The New Best of Fine Woodworking' book series. The series collects selected articles from Fine Woodworking Magazine over the past decade, organized by topic. The reviewer finds that the topical grouping adds value even for existing magazine subscribers. Despite minor editing errors, the books are recommended for both new and experienced woodworkers seeking consolidated reference material on specific techniques.
key_points: The book series compiles a decade of Fine Woodworking articles organized by specific topics like finishing, router techniques, and shop layout
Grouping articles by subject adds value beyond the original magazines by enabling focused exploration of a single topic
Minor editing errors exist but don't interfere with comprehension, and outdated pricing/brands are helpfully marked
The series serves both beginners seeking foundational knowledge and experienced woodworkers looking for alternative approaches
money_quotes: Taunton has always managed to bring a deep sense of quality to everything they produce.
Having all these articles grouped together by subject made it a joy to explore a topic.
By combining tidbits from different articles, I was able to pick up several tips and details to make me feel comfortable with trying a new finish.
For the old dog looking to learn a new trick, it's great to be able to thumb through one volume with a variety of alternatives and viewpoints on a topic.
I've never been disappointed with Taunton's offerings.
key_insight: Curating and organizing existing content by topic creates new value beyond the sum of its parts, making reference material more accessible and actionable for practitioners at all levels.
